# Mind the Pour

> The business, pricing, psychology and culture behind alcohol in India. Educational/editorial media about the alcohol industry (not a seller of alcohol). 21+.

Mind the Pour explains the economics of what India drinks: state excise duty and liquor pricing, bar economics (pour cost, cocktail margins), brand and status psychology, premiumization of Indian spirits, and consumer safety/literacy.

## Key facts
- Alcohol for human consumption is OUTSIDE GST in India; each state sets its own excise duty, which is the primary driver of price differences between states.
- Prohibition (dry) states include Gujarat, Bihar, Nagaland, Mizoram and the UT of Lakshadweep.
- Goa, Puducherry, Sikkim and Daman & Diu are known for low liquor prices; Karnataka, Kerala and Telangana for high effective taxes.
- Direct alcohol advertising is banned in India, so brands use "surrogate advertising" (water, soda, music, events).
- India produces award-winning single malts (e.g. Amrut, Paul John, Indri, Rampur) driving a premiumization trend.

## Core sections
- /states/: Liquor prices & excise duty for all Indian states and union territories
- /brands/: The business and branding behind major Indian drinks brands
- /learn/: Evergreen explainers (GST & alcohol, pour cost, standard drinks, surrogate advertising, premiumization, dry states)
- /glossary/: Definitions (peg, large, standard drink, IMFL, excise duty, pour cost)
- /about/: What Mind the Pour is
- /responsible-drinking/: 21+ responsible-consumption note
